paper_title: The Canonical Build Plan: Adversarial Optimization Through Folded Meta-Kaizen — A Formal Theory of Monotonic Quality Improvement in Multi-Agent Knowledge Production
paper_type_note: Primary contribution is the formalization of the CBP as a governance process with proved monotonic quality improvement. Contains both derivational results (Theorems 1-3, Proposition 4) and methodology specification (5-step process definition).

phase_ready.note: Draft produced under CBP Step 2. Awaiting Step 3 (hostile review by Grok) and Steps 4-5 before PHASE-READY declaration. One placeholder: PH-MK7.1 (full 70-claim corpus review in progress).

dependencies.role: KVS axioms A1-A6, KVS formula derivation (Theorem 4.1), threshold κ=0.50 derivation, self-referential applicability (Theorem 4.2)
DRS for software — first CBP application to executable substrates

claim_registry.label: Popperian Epistemological Foundation
claim_registry.statement: Falsificationism: we can falsify claims but not verify them. All Type F claims must carry deterministic falsification predicates.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Popper (1959). Standard epistemological foundation for the DRS framework.
claim_registry.note: Type A: foundational axiom shared with DRP-1.

claim_registry.label: Information-Theoretic Independence
claim_registry.statement: Systems with different architectures and training corpora produce partially independent error distributions: ρ(E₁, E₂) < 1.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: Ensemble diversity theory (Krogh & Vedelsby 1995). Empirically grounded in ML ensemble literature.
claim_registry.note: Type A: empirically motivated axiom. Could in principle be tested by comparing Claude-Grok error correlations.

claim_registry.label: KVS Axioms Received
claim_registry.statement: MK-P1 axioms A1-A6 and the derived KVS formula KVS = N × I' × C' × T are received as foundation. The threshold κ = 0.50 is derived from Bayesian decision theory under symmetric loss.
claim_registry.axiomatic_basis: MK-P1 Theorem 4.1 and Section 4.3. Received as Type A from MK-P1.
claim_registry.note: Type A: received result from MK-P1.

claim_registry.label: Canonical Build Plan Definition
claim_registry.statement: The CBP is a 5-step governance process: (1) First Build Plan, (2) Meta-Kaizen Pre-Optimization, (3) Adversarial Hostile Review, (4) Meta-Kaizen Post-Repair, (5) Final Build Plan. Meta-Kaizen appears twice — before and after the hostile review — creating a folded optimization.
claim_registry.note: Type D: definitional. The 5-step structure is a specification, not an empirical claim.

claim_registry.label: Quality Function Q
claim_registry.statement: Q: WorkProduct → ℝ is defined operationally as the proportion of falsifiable claims surviving independent falsification testing. Q(w) = |{claims not falsified}| / n.
claim_registry.note: Type D: operational definition. Q is a design choice, not a discovery.

claim_registry.label: Adversarial Information
claim_registry.statement: Information I_H generated by a hostile reviewer whose objective function is to falsify the work product's claims, as opposed to improve them.

claim_registry.label: Folded Governance
claim_registry.statement: A governance process P is folded if P = O₂ ∘ A ∘ O₁ where O₁ and O₂ are optimization passes using the same framework and A is an adversarial transformation between them.

claim_registry.label: Architecture Independence
claim_registry.statement: Two systems S₁ and S₂ are architecture-independent if their error distributions E₁ and E₂ satisfy ρ(E₁, E₂) < 1.
claim_registry.note: Type D: definition of the independence condition required for Theorem 2.

claim_registry.label: Monotonic Quality
claim_registry.statement: The CBP 5-step process produces outputs Q(Step 5) ≥ Q(Step 1) whenever the KVS threshold κ gates all modifications.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: A work product is exhibited where faithful application of all 5 CBP steps, with KVS ≥ κ gating enforced at Steps 2 and 4, produces a Final Build Plan (Step 5) that scores strictly lower on Q (proportion of claims surviving independent falsification) than the First Build Plan (Step 1).
falsification_predicate.WHERE: The work product contains ≥ 5 falsifiable claims. The independent falsification testing is conducted by a system not involved in either authoring or hostile review. Faithful application means all 5 steps are executed and all KVS scores are computed and documented.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Q(Step 5) < Q(Step 1) computed on the same falsification test suite, administered by the same independent evaluator, within the same evaluation session.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: κ = 0.50 and standard KVS formula from MK-P1 used without modification. Q is the operational definition in D-MK7.2.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Formal consequence of the KVS threshold derivation. Proof assumes KVS scoring applied faithfully — human or AI override invalidates the gate condition.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: A work product where the hostile reviewer identifies a critical flaw, the repair modification scores KVS = 0.55 (above threshold), but the repair introduces a new error that falsifies a previously-passing claim. Q(Step 5) = (n-1)/n < n/n = Q(Step 1). This is logically possible and would falsify the claim.
claim_registry.note: Core claim. The vacuity witness shows this is falsifiable: a repair that fixes one defect while introducing another could reduce Q.

claim_registry.label: Adversarial Detection Advantage
claim_registry.statement: Hostile review by an architecture-independent system detects defects not detectable by author self-review: |D₂ \ D₁| > 0.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: In ≥ 10 CBP applications with architecture-independent hostile review, the reviewer identifies zero unique defects (defects not already identified by author self-review) across all applications.
falsification_predicate.WHERE: Architecture independence verified: S₁ and S₂ have different model architectures and different training corpora. Both systems review the same work products. Unique defect means a defect identified by S₂ that S₁ did not identify when reviewing the same work product.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Count |D₂ \ D₁| across ≥ 10 applications. If Σ|D₂ \ D₁| = 0 at p < 0.05 (binomial test with H₀: P(unique defect per application) > 0), the claim is falsified.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Architecture independence requires different model families (e.g., Claude and Grok), not different versions of the same model.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Direct consequence of error distribution non-identity under architecture independence.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: 10 CBP applications where Claude self-review finds all defects that Grok also finds, plus additional defects Grok misses, yielding |D₂ \ D₁| = 0 for all 10. Logically possible if Claude's error distribution happens to dominate Grok's.
claim_registry.note: Empirically testable via the relay pipeline.

claim_registry.label: Novelty Amplification
claim_registry.statement: Post-hostile Meta-Kaizen (Step 4) produces higher-novelty improvement candidates than pre-hostile Meta-Kaizen (Step 2): E[N_Step4] > E[N_Step2].

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: Across ≥ 10 CBP applications, mean Novelty score N of Step 4 candidates ≤ mean Novelty score N of Step 2 candidates at p < 0.05 (two-sample t-test).
falsification_predicate.WHERE: Novelty N computed per MK-P1 Section 4.1 (Jaccard distance). Step 2 and Step 4 candidates from the same CBP application on the same work product.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Paired comparison of N scores within each CBP application. Mean difference computed across ≥ 10 applications.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: N computed using the same rolling keyword window for both passes within each application.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: The hostile review introduces new domain keywords, mechanically increasing Jaccard distance for post-hostile candidates.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: 10 CBP applications where hostile review raises only objections within the author's existing keyword set, yielding no new domain keywords and N_Step4 ≤ N_Step2.
claim_registry.note: Testable using existing corpus data from CBP applications.

claim_registry.label: Threshold Non-Degradation
claim_registry.statement: The KVS threshold κ ≥ 0.50 applied at both Meta-Kaizen passes prevents quality degradation: no accepted modification (KVS ≥ κ) reduces Q.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: A modification with KVS ≥ κ is exhibited that, when applied, reduces the work product's Q score.
falsification_predicate.WHERE: The modification is scored using standard KVS (MK-P1). The quality measurement is conducted by an independent evaluator before and after the modification.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Q_after < Q_before for a specific modification with KVS ≥ κ.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Uses κ = 0.50 and standard KVS without regime adjustment (MK-P4 KVS-hat excluded).
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Tests the core assumption of the threshold derivation: KVS ≥ κ implies non-negative expected value. A single counterexample falsifies.

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: A modification scored KVS = 0.60 that simplifies a claim's wording (appearing to improve clarity) but inadvertently removes a boundary condition, making the claim unfalsifiable. Q drops because the claim fails C6 vacuity. Logically possible.
claim_registry.note: Strong claim — a single counterexample suffices.

claim_registry.label: Multi-Architecture Amplification
claim_registry.statement: When hostile review is conducted by an architecture-independent system, defect detection rate D_cross exceeds same-architecture rate D_same.

falsification_predicate.FALSIFIED_IF: Parallel hostile reviews — one by the authoring architecture, one by an independent architecture — on ≥ 5 work products yield D_cross ≤ D_same at p < 0.05 (paired t-test).
falsification_predicate.WHERE: Same-architecture review means a different instance of the same model family reviews the work. Cross-architecture review means a model from a different family. Both reviews blind to each other.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: D_cross and D_same are unique defect counts per work product. Paired comparison across ≥ 5 products.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Architecture families must be genuinely different (e.g., Claude vs. Grok), not fine-tuned variants.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: Governance application of ensemble diversity principle (Krogh & Vedelsby 1995).

claim_registry.vacuity_witness: 5 work products where Claude self-review finds more defects than Grok cross-review, yielding D_cross < D_same. Possible if Grok's review is shallow or if Claude's self-knowledge compensates for error correlation.
claim_registry.note: Empirically testable via relay pipeline with parallel review protocol.

placeholders.description: Full 70-claim corpus review by Grok in progress. 20 of 70 claims reviewed. Preliminary results support F-MK7.2 and F-MK7.5 but sample size insufficient for statistical significance.

inference_rule_table.name: Threshold Gate Monotonicity
inference_rule_table.description: If every modification passes KVS ≥ κ, and κ is the indifference threshold, then the composition of modifications has non-negative quality contribution.

inference_rule_table.name: Error Distribution Non-Identity
inference_rule_table.description: If ρ(E₁, E₂) < 1, then D₂ \ D₁ ≠ ∅: the second system detects at least some defects the first cannot.

inference_rule_table.name: Information Set Enrichment
inference_rule_table.description: If MK₂ operates after hostile review H, it has access to information I_H not available to MK₁. Therefore E[Q(MK₂∘H∘MK₁)] > E[Q(MK₁∘MK₂)] when I_H is non-trivial.
